title: Toroidal Wrapping, Object Bounds, Viewports, Resize, and Scaling · Independent Program Trial
summary: An independent adaptive-arcade team receives a different viewport, device, frame rate, seed, entity count, accessibility profile, platform, and delivery constraint and transfers toroidal wrapping, object bounds, viewports, resize, and scaling into a changed seam-safe logical viewport and resize policy. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of seam-safe logical viewport and resize policy with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explic…
objectives: Choose center, radius, Rect, FRect, and visual bounds deliberately and keep float world state separate from raster bounds.
Wrap complete object bounds with modular arithmetic and test overshoot across corners and large time steps.
Compare shortest toroidal displacement or ghost proxies so collisions remain continuous across seams.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Center Vs Bounds
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Asteroids team handles case 4688 during a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial. Build deterministic Python evidence to wrap full object bounds across seams while preserving logical world state through resize and display scaling; preserve explicit pixels, seconds, radians or degrees, frames, entity counts, collision units, and budgets; then inject a 400 millisecond frame stall and prove the update budget prevents a spiral of death. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Center Vs Bounds.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for choose center, radius, rect, frect, and visual bounds deliberately and keep float world state separate from raster bounds. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Center Vs Bounds, keep Center Vs Bounds connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-resize-dpi-49vk54
# Competency: asteroids-resize-dpi.
# Case 7632: a controller-remapping accessibility review.
# Operating constraint: test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases.
# Changed case: run the packaged build with dummy video and audio drivers, missing optional sound, and a corrupted save.
def evidence_asteroids_resize_dpi_49vk54(x: float = -4.0, y: float = 605.0, width: float = 800.0, height: float = 600.0, radius: float = 12.0, resize_preserves_world: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_49vk54 = "49vk54-45kv94-7632"
    assert evidence_variant_49vk54
    evidence_axes_49vk54 = (
        "player49vk54",
        "seed7632",
        "input45kv94",
        "frame49vk547632",
        "state763249vk54",
        "playtest45kv947632",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_49vk54)) == 6
    if width <= 0.0 or height <= 0.0 or radius < 0.0:
        return False, width, "viewport-domain"
    wrapped_x = ((x + radius) % (width + 2 * radius)) - radius
    wrapped_y = ((y + radius) % (height + 2 * radius)) - radius
    if not resize_preserves_world:
        return False, wrapped_x, "resize-teleport"
    if not (-radius <= wrapped_x < width + radius):
        return False, wrapped_x, "wrap-x"
    assert -radius <= wrapped_y < height + radius
    return True, round(wrapped_x, 3), "seam-safe-wrap"
